packs.name: Quick Start
packs.description: Standard Turbo model with full pipeline — ready to generate in minutes. Best for most users.

packs.name: Minimal Setup
packs.description: Smallest viable setup — great for low-VRAM GPUs or quick testing.

packs.name: XL Quality
packs.description: XL Turbo model — larger architecture for richer, more detailed audio. Needs ~12GB VRAM.

packs.name: Blackwell Optimized
packs.description: MXFP4 quantised XL — native FP4 Tensor Core acceleration on RTX 5000 series. 22% faster than Q4.

packs.name: CUDA 13 Runtime
packs.description: NVIDIA CUDA 13.x runtime libraries required for GPU-accelerated generation. ~510 MB download.

packs.name: CUDA 12 Runtime
packs.description: NVIDIA CUDA 12.x runtime libraries for the CUDA 12.8 compatibility build. ~810 MB download.

packs.name: Stem Separation
packs.description: All 4 models for multi-stem audio separation in Cover Studio. Required for the SuperSep pipeline.

packs.name: Stem Separation Runtime
packs.description: ONNX Runtime + cuDNN libraries required to run SuperSep stem separation on GPU. ~1.3 GB download.

Base model in MXFP4 — 3.7x compression, native FP4 on Blackwell GPUs.
SFT model in MXFP4 — strong structure with minimal VRAM.
SFT+Turbo merge in MXFP4 — balanced and tiny.
Turbo in MXFP4 — fastest possible generation with minimal VRAM.
Continuous generation variant in MXFP4.
Shift1 scheduling in MXFP4.
Shift3 scheduling in MXFP4.
XL Base in MXFP4 — large model, small footprint.
XL SFT in MXFP4 — best structure with low VRAM.
XL SFT+Turbo merge in MXFP4.
XL Turbo in MXFP4 — fastest XL with Blackwell native FP4 acceleration.
Base+Turbo merge in MXFP4.
SFT+Turbo (mostly SFT) merge in MXFP4.
SFT+Turbo (mostly Turbo) merge in MXFP4.
Full-size language model — best audio code generation quality. 7.9GB.
Near-lossless 4B LM — recommended balance of quality and size.
4B LM with good quality at reduced size.
4B LM — smaller with some quality trade-off.
Mid-size LM — good quality with lower VRAM. 3.5GB. ⚠️ Not compatible with Vulkan builds (use 4B instead).
Mid-size LM quantised — decent quality, very compact. ⚠️ Not compatible with Vulkan builds (use 4B instead).
Smallest LM — fast but lower quality audio codes. 1.3GB. ⚠️ Not compatible with Vulkan builds (use 4B instead).
Tiny LM — minimum viable for quick experiments. ⚠️ Not compatible with Vulkan builds (use 4B instead).
Full-precision text encoder — architecturally locked to all DiT models.
Quantised text encoder — recommended, minimal quality loss.
Original ACE-Step VAE decoder — always BF16, required for all generation.
Fine-tuned VAE decoder with +38% high-frequency energy, +29dB dynamic range. Drop-in replacement for clearer, brighter audio.
Original ACE-Step VAE in safetensors format. Full F32 precision — better encode quality for cover/restyle vs GGUF.
ScragVAE in safetensors format. Full F32 precision + reduced artifacts. Best quality for cover/restyle.
DreamVAE in ONNX format — alternative decoder architecture, runs via ORT decode path.
Regrind V9b — latest iteration with refined high-frequency response and reduced artifacts.
Regrind V9b Blend50 — 50/50 blend of Regrind V9b with stock weights for balanced clarity.
Post-processing VAE (full precision) — neural audio polish via encode→decode round-trip. Recommended for best cleanup quality.
Post-processing VAE (half precision) — good balance of quality and size.
Post-processing VAE (FP16) — alternative half-precision variant.
Stable Audio 3 diffusion transformer graph — weights live in the companion .data file.
Stable Audio 3 diffusion transformer external weights — the largest file in the set.
Stable Audio autoencoder (encoder half) — encodes the instrumental into SA3 latents.
Stable Audio autoencoder (decoder half) — decodes refined SA3 latents back to audio.
T5Gemma text encoder — turns the refine prompt into conditioning embeddings.
Duration conditioning embedder — tells SA3 the target clip length.
T5Gemma SentencePiece tokenizer — required for prompt tokenization.
Tokenizer configuration for the T5Gemma tokenizer.
Special token definitions for the T5Gemma tokenizer.
GGML backend: Stable Audio 3 diffusion transformer (BF16 GGUF). Runs via GGML on CUDA, Vulkan or CPU — the fastest option on NVIDIA in current testing.
GGML backend: Stable Audio autoencoder (encoder half, F16 GGUF) — encodes the instrumental into SA3 latents.
GGML backend: Stable Audio autoencoder (decoder half, F16 GGUF) — decodes refined SA3 latents back to audio.
GGML backend: T5Gemma text encoder (BF16 GGUF) — turns the refine prompt into conditioning embeddings.
Stage 1: Primary 6-stem separation (vocals, drums, bass, guitar, piano, other). BS-Roformer architecture.
Stage 2: Vocal sub-separation into lead and backing vocals. Mel-Band RoFormer architecture.
Stage 3: Drum sub-separation (kick, snare, toms, hi-hat, cymbals). MDX23C architecture.
Stage 4: 'Other' stem refinement via Hybrid Transformer Demucs. Meta Research architecture.
Core ONNX Runtime inference library. Required for all SuperSep stem separation.
CUDA execution provider for GPU-accelerated stem separation.
Shared provider support library for ONNX Runtime.
NVIDIA cuDNN core library.
cuDNN advanced operations (attention, RNN, etc.).
cuDNN convolution operations.
Pre-compiled cuDNN engine kernels for optimal GPU performance.
Runtime-compiled cuDNN engine kernels.
cuDNN Tensor IR engines.
cuDNN graph operations and fusion.
cuDNN heuristic engine selection.
cuDNN base operations library.
NVIDIA cuBLAS library — required for CUDA 13.x accelerated matrix operations.
NVIDIA cuBLAS Light library — CUDA 13.x extended matrix operation support.
NVIDIA CUDA 13.x Runtime library — required for GPU initialisation.
NVIDIA cuBLAS library — required for CUDA 12.x accelerated matrix operations.
NVIDIA cuBLAS Light library — CUDA 12.x extended matrix operation support.
NVIDIA CUDA 12.x Runtime library — required for GPU initialisation.
Best balance of speed and accuracy (1.5 GB). Recommended. ~5-10s per 30s track on GPU.
Maximum accuracy (2.9 GB). Slower but best for difficult transcriptions. ~10-20s per 30s track on GPU.
Good accuracy with lower VRAM usage (1.4 GB). ~3-8s per 30s track on GPU.
Fastest, smallest (141 MB). Good for quick previews but lower accuracy. ~1-3s per 30s track on GPU.
